---
title: "Nested Receptive Field Protocol"
description: "Adaptive electrophysiology toolkit that dynamically generates stimuli based on real-time neural response analysis."
categories: [MATLAB, Python, Signal Processing, Experimental Design]
priority: 4
---

## Overview

Designed and built an adaptive experimental protocol for electrophysiology recordings. The system runs a coarse mapping protocol, automatically analyzes the neural responses, then generates a tailored high-resolution follow-up protocol centered on each neuron's identified receptive field. This two-stage adaptive approach reduces experimental time and improves data quality by focusing resources where they matter most.

## Technical Highlights

- **228 commits** of iterative development
- **Adaptive pipeline design**: automated analysis of protocol 1 results feeds directly into protocol 2 generation
- **Real-time signal processing** of electrophysiology data
- **MATLAB GUI** for parameter input and experiment control
- **Automated protocol generation** from prior experimental results — no manual intervention required between stages

## Technologies

[MATLAB]{.tag} [Python]{.tag} [Signal Processing]{.tag} [Experimental Design]{.tag} [Real-time Analysis]{.tag}

## Links

- [Source Code]({{< var repos.nested_rf >}})
- [Documentation]({{< var repos.reiser_docs_site >}}/Ephys/ephys_nested_rf.html)
